frame_analyses.description: Title slide 'Behind the Knife' with red background - instructional content intro
Text slide describing 'Two-Handed Tie (left hand)' requirements: knot tying board, practice string/suture, gloves
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Listing equipment and materials needed for training

frame_analyses.description: Overhead view of knot tying board with blue suture, brass clamps, and gloved hand visible on left side
Close-up of knot tying board with blue suture in position, brass clamps visible, yellow suture clips showing mark positions
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Preparing for demonstration of two-handed tie technique

frame_analyses.description: Both hands visible holding blue suture, demonstrating initial L-shape formation with fingers positioned around suture
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hands positioned to create the 'L' configuration described in verbal instruction

frame_analyses.description: Hands demonstrating suture crossing motion - right side of suture crossing over left index finger to form 'X'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear demonstration of the crossing technique for first throw formation

frame_analyses.description: Hand position showing index finger and thumb rotation - preparing to cinch the first throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrating the rotation where index finger moves to top and thumb to bottom position

frame_analyses.description: Close-up of hand executing the cinch motion - squeezing index and thumb together to tighten knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows proper tension application and knot formation

frame_analyses.description: Hands repositioning for second throw - forming new 'C' shape configuration with both hands, suture draped around thumbs
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Transitioning between throws with proper hand positioning

frame_analyses.description: Demonstrating crossing motion for second throw with left side of suture crossing over left thumb
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Showing alternating hand lead from first throw

frame_analyses.description: Final hand position showing completed knot with all three throws visible on blue suture, both hands releasing tension
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completed knot demonstrates secure tying with proper hand technique throughout

frame_analyses.description: Text slide beginning 'Two-Handed Tie (left hand) Review' with first instruction point visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational review of key technique points

frame_analyses.description: Continuation of review slide with detailed steps for L-formation and X-crossing technique
Final review slide showing C-formation and crossing instructions for subsequent throws

confidence_rationale: This is an instructional/educational video demonstrating two-handed knot tying technique with narration, text overlays, and staged demonstrations. It is NOT a performance video and therefore does NOT receive an FLS score. The content is educational and serves to teach the technique, not to assess trainee performance.
cannot_determine: Actual performance assessment - this is instructional content, not a trainee performance
Actual completion time - video demonstrates technique, not task completion
Penalties - no task execution occurs that would incur penalties

demonstrated_techniques: Two-handed tie formation with left hand leading
L-shape configuration with index fingers and suture
Crossing right side of suture over left index finger to form X
Index-thumb rotation (index on top, thumb on bottom)
Cinching motion to tighten first throw
C-shape configuration with both hands for second throw
Suture draped around thumbs for secure hold
Proper tension application throughout knot formation
verbal_cues: Start by making L's with both hands and drape suture around index fingers
Cross right side of suture over left index finger to make an X
Pinch index finger and thumb together and rotate so index is on top and thumb on bottom
Pick up both ends of suture in same hands as before
Make C's with both hands and suture draped around thumbs
Cross right side of suture over left thumb to make an X
Re-pinch thumb and index finger together with right side of suture in-between
common_errors_addressed: Improper hand positioning leading to weak knots
Failure to alternate hand leads between throws
Loss of control of suture ends during transitions

video_type_notes: This is a structured educational video from 'Behind the Knife' series that teaches fundamental two-handed tie technique. It includes equipment overview, live demonstration, and detailed review with step-by-step instructions. The video is designed for training purposes and does not represent an actual FLS task performance assessment.
